req.title: Submit leave request — variant 5
req.statement: A full-time employee shall be able to submit a leave request for between 1 and 30 business days; requests spanning more than 30 days require hr-admin approval. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.
preconditions: authenticated
employment_type:full_time
error_pathways: 422 when request exceeds 30 days without hr-admin

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines a leave-request submission flow with: (1) a valid range of 1-30 business days for self-approval, (2) >30 days requiring hr-admin approval, (3) 2FA confirmation mandatory, and (4) three explicit error pathways (400, 409, 422). Test selection covers: EP for a nominal in-range request; BVA for the exact 30-day upper boundary; NEG/NEG for start>end (400) and overlapping leave (409); DT for the >30 days without hr-admin (422) decision-table rule — this test is negative kind paired with DT heuristic because the DT condition combination (employee + days>30 + no hr-admin) yields an error outcome; STC for the state transition from draft to pending after form submission. The violation in test[5] was that a negative kind was paired with STC heuristic — STC is reserved for state-transition coverage which is a structural/positive technique. The fix reassigns test[5] (the 422 scenario) from heuristic=STC to heuristic=DT, which correctly models the decision-table condition combination driving the error pathway.

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_valid_mid_range
preconditions: User is authenticated as a full-time employee
2FA is enrolled and OTP is available
No overlapping leave exists for the chosen date range
Chosen date range spans 10 business days (well within 1-30 partition)
actions: Fill start_date and end_date covering 10 business days
expected: Leave request record created in DB
tests.executable:
def test_submit_leave_request_valid_mid_range(page, auth_full_time_employee, otp_provider, api_client):
    start, end = business_day_range(days=10)
    page.goto("/leave-requests/new")
    page.fill("[data-testid='start-date']", start)
    page.fill("[data-testid='end-date']", end)
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")
    otp = otp_provider.current_otp(auth_full_time_employee["totp_secret"])
    page.fill("[data-testid='otp-input']", otp)
    page.click("[data-testid='otp-confirm-btn']")
    assert page.locator("[data-testid='success-banner']").is_visible()
    leave = api_client.get_latest_leave(auth_full_time_employee["id"])
    assert leave["status"] == "pending"
    assert leave["start_date"] == start
    notifications = api_client.get_manager_notifications(auth_full_time_employee["manager_id"])
    assert any(n["leave_id"] == leave["id"] for n in notifications)
    emails = get_sent_emails(auth_full_time_employee["email"])
    assert len(emails) >= 1

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_exactly_30_days_boundary
preconditions: User is authenticated as a full-time employee
2FA is enrolled and OTP is available
No overlapping leave exists for the chosen date range
Chosen date range spans exactly 30 business days (upper boundary)
actions: Fill start_date and end_date covering exactly 30 business days
expected: HTTP 201 / success page shown (no hr-admin approval required at boundary)
trace: R-WEB-061: upper boundary BVA — 30 days is last self-approval value
R-WEB-061: >30 days triggers 422 / hr-admin path; =30 must not
tests.executable:
def test_submit_leave_request_exactly_30_days_boundary(page, auth_full_time_employee, otp_provider, api_client):
    start, end = business_day_range(days=30)
    page.goto("/leave-requests/new")
    page.fill("[data-testid='start-date']", start)
    page.fill("[data-testid='end-date']", end)
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")
    otp = otp_provider.current_otp(auth_full_time_employee["totp_secret"])
    page.fill("[data-testid='otp-input']", otp)
    page.click("[data-testid='otp-confirm-btn']")
    assert page.locator("[data-testid='success-banner']").is_visible()
    leave = api_client.get_latest_leave(auth_full_time_employee["id"])
    assert leave["status"] == "pending"
    assert leave["business_days"] == 30

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_start_after_end_returns_400
preconditions: User is authenticated as a full-time employee
2FA is enrolled
actions: Fill start_date with a date after end_date
expected: Error message indicates start_date must not exceed end_date
trace: R-WEB-061 error pathway: 400 when start_date > end_date
tests.executable:
def test_submit_leave_request_start_after_end_returns_400(page, auth_full_time_employee, api_client):
    start = date_offset(days=10)
    end = date_offset(days=5)
    count_before = api_client.leave_count(auth_full_time_employee["id"])
    page.goto("/leave-requests/new")
    page.fill("[data-testid='start-date']", start)
    page.fill("[data-testid='end-date']", end)
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")
    error = page.locator("[data-testid='form-error']")
    assert error.is_visible()
    assert "start date" in error.inner_text().lower()
    assert api_client.leave_count(auth_full_time_employee["id"]) == count_before

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_overlapping_existing_returns_409
preconditions: User is authenticated as a full-time employee
2FA is enrolled
An approved leave already exists overlapping the target date range
actions: Fill start_date and end_date overlapping the existing leave
trace: R-WEB-061 error pathway: 409 when overlapping existing leave
tests.executable:
def test_submit_leave_request_overlapping_existing_returns_409(page, auth_full_time_employee, otp_provider, api_client):
    existing_start, existing_end = business_day_range(days=5)
    seed_approved_leave(auth_full_time_employee["id"], existing_start, existing_end)
    page.goto("/leave-requests/new")
    page.fill("[data-testid='start-date']", existing_start)
    page.fill("[data-testid='end-date']", existing_end)
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")
    otp = otp_provider.current_otp(auth_full_time_employee["totp_secret"])
    page.fill("[data-testid='otp-input']", otp)
    page.click("[data-testid='otp-confirm-btn']")
    error = page.locator("[data-testid='conflict-error']")
    assert error.is_visible()
    assert "overlap" in error.inner_text().lower()
    assert api_client.leave_count(auth_full_time_employee["id"]) == 1

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_exceeds_30_days_without_hr_admin_returns_422
preconditions: User is authenticated as a full-time employee (not hr-admin)
2FA is enrolled
No overlapping leave exists
Chosen date range spans 31 business days
actions: Fill start_date and end_date covering 31 business days
expected: UI displays message requiring hr-admin approval
Leave request not created in pending state
trace: R-WEB-061 error pathway: 422 when request exceeds 30 days without hr-admin
R-WEB-061: DT rule — actor=employee, days>30, no hr-admin => 422
tests.executable:
def test_submit_leave_request_exceeds_30_days_without_hr_admin_returns_422(page, auth_full_time_employee, otp_provider, api_client):
    start, end = business_day_range(days=31)
    page.goto("/leave-requests/new")
    page.fill("[data-testid='start-date']", start)
    page.fill("[data-testid='end-date']", end)
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")
    otp = otp_provider.current_otp(auth_full_time_employee["totp_secret"])
    page.fill("[data-testid='otp-input']", otp)
    page.click("[data-testid='otp-confirm-btn']")
    error = page.locator("[data-testid='hr-admin-required-error']")
    assert error.is_visible()
    assert "hr-admin" in error.inner_text().lower()
    leave = api_client.get_latest_leave(auth_full_time_employee["id"])
    assert leave is None or leave["status"] != "pending"

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_state_transition_draft_to_pending
preconditions: User is authenticated as a full-time employee
2FA is enrolled and OTP is available
No overlapping leave exists
Chosen date range spans 5 business days
actions: Verify leave form is in initial draft state
Fill start_date and end_date covering 5 business days
Retrieve the created leave record via API
expected: Leave record transitions from no state (draft UI) to status='pending' after successful submission
State 'pending' is reflected immediately in the API response
No intermediate error state is observed
trace: R-WEB-061: STC — state transition: draft form -> submitted -> pending
R-WEB-061: request created outcome; additional variant semantics honoured
tests.executable:
def test_submit_leave_request_state_transition_draft_to_pending(page, auth_full_time_employee, otp_provider, api_client):
    start, end = business_day_range(days=5)
    page.goto("/leave-requests/new")
    assert page.locator("[data-testid='leave-form']").is_visible()
    page.fill("[data-testid='start-date']", start)
    page.fill("[data-testid='end-date']", end)
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")
    otp = otp_provider.current_otp(auth_full_time_employee["totp_secret"])
    page.fill("[data-testid='otp-input']", otp)
    page.click("[data-testid='otp-confirm-btn']")
    assert page.locator("[data-testid='success-banner']").is_visible()
    leave = api_client.get_latest_leave(auth_full_time_employee["id"])
    assert leave is not None
    assert leave["status"] == "pending"
